beats.activity: Wake up, coffee on the porch watching the water and morning light
beats.reasoning: Felix is a creature of habit and early riser; this mirrors his ritual from recent days. Warm, clear Friday morning is perfect for his porch routine.
Tax season is still on; he drives to his accounting workplace. No transit strikes today, so standard Friday commute.

beats.activity: Client meetings, reconciliations, and tax preparation work
beats.reasoning: Tax season intensity continues; Friday morning is typically packed with end-of-week client calls and spreadsheet work before the weekend.

beats.activity: Walk to Cherry Street food truck rotation spot
beats.reasoning: Felix knows every food truck's rotation and makes a habit of lunch away from his desk; Cherry Street is his go-to spot.

beats.activity: Grab lunch from food truck and sit in the shade, people-watching
beats.reasoning: Warm Friday (77F) is perfect for outdoor lunch. Felix values these breaks from his desk to decompress during tax season.
Return to workplace for afternoon work session.

beats.activity: Afternoon tax work: client follow-ups, reconciliations, and compliance checks
beats.reasoning: Friday afternoon tax season push; Felix powers through remaining work to clear the desk before evening.

beats.activity: Drive home from First Hill, avoid Broadway/Pike closures due to Pride kickoff
beats.reasoning: Seattle Pride street fair and road closures start at 5pm on Broadway and Pike; Felix takes alternate route home to Madrona, adding buffer time.

beats.activity: Quick dinner at home, light meal to refuel
beats.reasoning: Friday evening; Felix prefers a light, simple meal after work before heading out for leisure activities.

beats.activity: Drive to pottery studio for evening session
beats.reasoning: Felix unwinds with pottery after work; warm, clear Friday evening is ideal. Capitol Hill studio is accessible despite nearby Pride closures on Broadway.

beats.activity: Pottery studio time: centering clay, throwing on wheel, creative unwinding
beats.reasoning: Felix's preferred way to shake off tax-season stress; the tactile, meditative work with clay quiets his mind after a long work week.
Return to Madrona after evening pottery; Pride festivities are winding down by late evening.

diary: Started the day right—coffee on the porch with the water glassy and golden. By the time I hit the office, tax season was in full swing: client calls, reconciliations, the usual grind. Cherry Street was a nice break; I grabbed a plate from the food truck and just watched people flow past. The afternoon blurred into more compliance work, but I managed to slip out before the Pride crowds swallowed downtown. Dinner was quick, then straight to the pottery studio. There's something about centering clay on the wheel that just... dissolves the day. My hands remembered the motion, and for two hours I wasn't thinking about tax codes or client deadlines. Drove home quiet through the cool night, feeling loose and present.
thought_process: I knew the Pride kickoff would clog the usual routes, so I made sure to leave early enough to avoid that mess—no point fighting traffic. The food truck lunch was intentional too; I needed a real break, not just wolfing something at my desk. Work was solid but draining, all those reconciliations piling up as June approaches. The pottery studio was non-negotiable tonight though. After eight hours of numbers and emails, I needed to make something with my hands, to feel like I was creating instead of just fixing. The warm weather and the fact that it's Friday definitely helped my mood shift—I could feel the season turning, people energized. By the time I was at the wheel, I wasn't fighting the day anymore; I was just present in it.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
